prose:
Method provenance (required details for reproducibility)

• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (nucleophilic attack), f⁻ (electrophilic attack), dual descriptor; identification of the most electrophilic and most nucleophilic atomic sites.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`), no density fitting (exact 4-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x.
• Geometry provenance: single-point population analyses on the user-supplied nitro­benzene geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`; no additional optimisation (`DFT//input geometry`).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); N–1 cation and N+1 anion states treated as doublets (multiplicity 2) per default.
• Software/backend: chemkit `fukui` skill (PySCF under the hood). Live log is streamed to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_o3/20260706-185025_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260706-185025.out` (tail this file for SCF details).
• Convergence & integrity: all SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true. Charge-conservation tests passed (∑f⁺ = ∑f⁻ = 1.0000). All warnings are reproduced verbatim below.

Key numerical results (Mulliken scheme)
• Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C), f⁺ = 0.49641781
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C), f⁻ = 0.1072882

C5 0.11563 / 0.10729 / 0.00834 … (full table in JSON result `nitrobenzene_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json`).
